title: Ripple Secures Full MiCA License for Crypto Services Across Europe
summary: Ripple has obtained a full MiCA license in Luxembourg, allowing it to offer regulated crypto services throughout the European Economic Area. This marks a significant step toward mainstream crypto adoption in Europe.
body:
Ripple, the blockchain company behind the XRP cryptocurrency, has secured a full MiCA (Markets in Crypto-Assets) license from Luxembourg's financial regulator. This approval enables Ripple to provide regulated crypto-asset services across all 30 countries in the European Economic Area (EEA).

The Luxembourg approval completes Ripple's MiCA licensing process, enabling it to offer services such as crypto trading, custody, and payment solutions under a unified regulatory framework. The MiCA regulation, which came into effect in late 2024, aims to create a harmonized framework for crypto-assets in the EU.

This development is significant for everyday crypto users in Europe, as it provides a more secure and regulated environment for crypto transactions. With Ripple's services now fully compliant with MiCA, users can expect greater transparency and protection when using Ripple's platforms. This could also encourage more traditional financial institutions to integrate crypto services, further bridging the gap between traditional finance and the crypto world.

For those interested in using Ripple's services, this license means that the company's offerings will now be subject to stricter regulatory oversight, potentially reducing risks associated with crypto investments. Users should watch for Ripple's official announcements regarding the launch of new services under the MiCA framework. Read more → https://cointelegraph.com/news/ripple-secures-full-mica-license-for-crypto-services-across-europe?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound
twitterThread: Ripple just got a full MiCA license in Luxembourg, meaning regulated crypto services across Europe. Here's what it means for you.

The MiCA license allows Ripple to offer crypto trading, custody, and payment solutions under a unified EU framework across 30 EEA countries. This could make crypto safer and more mainstream in Europe.

If you're in Europe, keep an eye out for Ripple's new compliant services. This is a big step toward regulated crypto adoption. Read more → https://cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-07-06-ripple-secures-full-mica-license-for-crypto-services-across-europe
standaloneTweet: Ripple's new MiCA license means regulated crypto services across all 30 EEA countries. Safer, more mainstream crypto is coming. Watch for new compliant services soon.
